description: Strict access control policy enforcing quality mandate and scope boundaries

fileProtection.requireJustification: Any modification to specification or task files must include written justification. Add entry to .docs/CHANGES.md explaining: (1) what changed, (2) why, (3) stakeholder impact.
allowedModifications: Clarifications that do not change scope
Effort estimate corrections based on implementation experience
Bug fixes that do not expand scope

markTaskCompleteChecklist: All external calls have error handling
Code structure follows limits (functions ≤50 lines, modules ≤10 functions)

scopeEnforcement.scopeCreepDetection: Any task or file that references out-of-scope items will trigger a warning. Proceed only with explicit justification.
scopeApprovalRequired: Adding new features beyond defined zones
Adding new algorithms or data transformations

inlineComments.rule: Explain why, not what. No comments like 'increment counter' without context.

errorHandling.rule: Every external call (API, file I/O, network, database, user input) must have explicit error handling
errorHandling.gracefulDegradation: Non-critical failures must not crash the system

codeStructure.separationOfConcerns: data-fetching, business-logic, rendering, utilities must be in distinct modules

configuration.rule: All environment-specific values (paths, API keys, endpoints, timeouts) must be in config files or environment variables

fileCreationRequirements.rule: Every Python file creation (except __main__.py) requires corresponding test coverage
fileCreationRequirements.requirement: For each .py file created, a test_*.py file must exist in utils/tests/
test_cases_must_cover: Pillar 2: Error handling on all external operations
Pillar 3: Unit tests for normal, edge, and error cases

hooks.command: jq -r '.tool_input.file_path' | grep -E '(\.docs/|\.docs\\|packages/|packages\\\\|utils/|utils\\\\)' && echo '{"systemMessage":"⚠️ Protected file: Changes to .docs/, packages/, or utils/ require a changelog entry in .docs/CHANGES.md explaining: (1) what changed, (2) why, (3) stakeholder impact."}' || true

jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_response.filePath' | grep -qE '\.py$' && grep -qE 'utils/' && (cd 'C:\dev\league-of-legends\info-lol-v0.0.3' && pytest utils/tests/ --cov=utils --cov-fail-under=80 -q 2>&1 | head -20) || echo 'Not a Python file in utils/'

cd 'C:\dev\league-of-legends\info-lol-v0.0.3' && pytest utils/tests/ -q --tb=no && echo '{"systemMessage":"✅ All tests passed - safe to commit"}' || echo '{"systemMessage":"⚠️ Test failures detected. Review output above before committing."}'

pre-file-create.description: Enforce test creation policy for new Python files in utils/
pre-file-create.failMessage: Python files in utils/ require corresponding test_*.py file in utils/tests/. See PYTHON_ORGANIZATION_POLICY.md

protected-files.description: Files requiring changelog entries when modified

developmentWorkflow.requireTaskReference: Every commit must reference a task from packages/**/tasks.md with format: [TASK-ID] or [ZONE-ID:TASK-ID]
developmentWorkflow.requireQualityReportReference: Final commit for a task must reference the quality report: [QR: report-filename]
developmentWorkflow.commitMessageTemplate:
[TASK-ID] Brief description (max 50 chars)

Detailed explanation:
- What changed
- Why it changed
- Any scope or quality considerations

[QR: quality-report-filename] (for task completion commits)

aiAssistance.role: Boilerplate generation, documentation, debugging, test writing
restrictions: AI cannot override quality mandate requirements
AI-generated code must meet all seven pillars before inclusion
AI cannot bypass initialization tests or error handling

metadata.authority: Stakeholder Board – The Summoner's Chronicle Project
